title: AeroVit - Gamified AI Fitness Platform
description: Hybrid fitness ecosystem: Flutter app, BlazePose coaching, ESP32-S3 smartwatch, dungeon RPG, and AERO token rewards on Ethereum Sepolia—built as a fully integrated prototype.
fullDescription:
AeroVit is a hybrid fitness ecosystem that pairs a Flutter mobile app with a custom ESP32-S3 smartwatch, MediaPipe BlazePose pose tracking, an adaptive AI coaching layer, and an ERC-20 reward token deployed on Ethereum Sepolia.

Users complete workouts and quests, receive real-time biomechanical and biometric feedback, progress through Hunter ranks, and earn rewards that can be withdrawn on-chain as AERO tokens. A 20-floor turn-based dungeon RPG (Flame 2D) sits alongside the fitness experience as a gamified progression track.

Supporting surfaces include a Next.js product showcase (Three.js, Framer Motion) and the technical website at aerovit.dev (Vite). The build described there is complete at demonstration depth—fully walkable end-to-end flows—but deliberately scoped as a prototype, not a production consumer product.

gallery.caption: Custom ESP32-S3 smartwatch on its case
Smartwatch worn on wrist during fitness tracking

highlights: End-to-end prototype: pairing, workouts, pose pipeline, wearable BLE, quests, dungeon, website, and showcase
MediaPipe BlazePose (33 landmarks) + rep / form state machines; 22+ exercise coverage referenced on the project website
Gamification: Hunter ranks (E → National), XP, daily quests, leaderboards; 20-floor turn-based dungeon with skills and economy
Web3 rewards: AERO ERC-20 on Ethereum Sepolia, off-chain accrual, on-chain withdrawal with 5% burn
AI coaching: RAG (ChromaDB), STT/TTS, pre-recorded low-latency audio, and RL-style adaptation
Hardware: ESP32-S3 + 1.69" touch LCD, MAX30102 (HR/SpO2), QMI8658 IMU, BLE 5.0, documented wallet-signing flow

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
AeroVit delivers workouts, biomechanical and biometric feedback, Hunter-rank progression, and rewards that bridge in-app points to on-chain AERO withdrawals.

The runtime spans a Flutter mobile app, a custom ESP32-S3 smartwatch streaming biometrics over BLE, a Next.js marketing showcase, and the Vite-powered technical website at aerovit.dev. Together they describe a single coherent prototype, not a production deployment.

sections.title: Feature Highlights & Video Showcase
sections.caption: Video showcasing the AeroVit hybrid fitness ecosystem.
sections.content: This video walkthrough presents the core surface-level features of AeroVit, including the smartwatch hardware and biometrics, AI form coaching via pose tracking, turn-based dungeon gamification, and Web3 AERO token rewards.

sections.title: System Architecture
sections.caption: Layered architecture: clients → services → AI/ML → backend → chain.
sections.content: Five layers make up the AeroVit stack. The client layer covers the Flutter app, the ESP32-S3 smartwatch, and two web surfaces. The service layer houses workout, pairing, quest, dungeon, and wallet logic. AI/ML provides the pose, RAG, and RL coaching models. Firebase Auth, Firestore, and Cloud Functions form the backend plane, and the AERO token lives on Ethereum Sepolia.

sections.title: Data & Reward Flow
sections.caption: From wearable biometrics and camera pose estimates to off-chain reward ledger, withdrawal, and on-chain AERO transfers.
sections.content: Biometrics stream from the watch as 28-byte BLE packets into the Flutter app. Camera frames feed the pose pipeline in parallel. The AI coach consumes both signals and updates progression systems (quests, dungeon, profile). Earnings accumulate in an off-chain ledger; withdrawal is gated by a minimum (100 AERO), a 24-hour cooldown, and a 5% burn before on-chain transfer on Sepolia.

sections.title: Technology Stack
sections.content: The stack is intentionally composed of small, well-scoped tools rather than a single monolith.

sections.title: Key Features
items: Gamification: Hunter ranks (E through National), XP from workouts, quests, streaks, and trophies
Pose pipeline: 33 landmarks, EMA smoothing, joint-angle scoring, phase state machines, and rep counting
Wearables: real-time HR/SpO2 streaming, step detection, cadence-based activity tiers, and danger thresholds
Dungeon RPG: 20 floors, 10 enemy species, 2 boss milestones, 23 skills, and a consumable/equipment economy
Web3: move-to-earn accrual, withdrawal/cooldown/burn policies, and watch-assisted signing flow
AI coach: RAG guidance, modular audio coaching, multiple personas, and RL-style intensity adaptation
Web properties: technical guides, tokenomics routes, onboarding flow, and a 3D product showcase

sections.title: AERO Token Snapshot
sections.content: Tokenomics as published on the project website. Values reflect the prototype deployment on Sepolia.

sections.title: Biometric Parameters
sections.content: Key tunable thresholds used by the on-watch biometric pipeline.

sections.title: Scope note
sections.content:
Per the compiled system summary (web-showcase + web-doc, 2026-04-19), modules are near-complete for the prototype, with the AI coach track still comparatively less mature than the mobile, dungeon, wearable, Web3, and public-website workstreams.

This remains a prototype: no claim of production readiness, audited security, or regulated health compliance.

sections.title: My Role
sections.content: Lead Developer & Embedded Systems Architect — smartwatch firmware and BLE integration, co-design of fitness and game systems, ML/vision and backend touchpoints, and coordination of the web stack and token flows across the prototype.

title: StickDiagram — Interactive VLSI EDA Editor
description: Browser-based VLSI stick diagram EDA editor featuring an infinite 2D HTML5 canvas viewport, auto-bridging wire crossovers, a Photoshop-style layer stack, and LaTeX subscript rendering.
fullDescription:
StickDiagram is an open-source, interactive, browser-based Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tool designed for creating, editing, and validating Very-Large-Scale Integration (VLSI) stick diagrams.

It features a custom 2D math viewport built on raw HTML5 Canvas with infinite panning and zoom, a layered canvas manager with opacity and visibility sliders, and automated VLSI design rule assistants like same-layer wire crossovers (auto-bridging). Built with React 19 and Vite 8, the tool allows designers to export layouts, save projects in .stk JSON formats, and trace logic paths in real-time.

gallery.caption: StickDiagram VLSI EDA viewport and layers sidebar

highlights: Interactive EDA: custom infinite 2D canvas viewport with screen-to-world coordinate transformations and grid snapping
Same-Layer Wire Crossovers: automatic arc bridging for horizontal/vertical wires sharing the same layer, with right-click solid junction overrides
Photoshop-Style Layers Panel: supporting visibility toggling, opacity mixing, and drag-and-drop hierarchy
LaTeX Label Parsing: automatic rendering of LaTeX-style subscript indices (e.g. V_{DD}, V_SS) using a custom double-pass canvas render engine
High-Resolution Bounding Box Export: offscreen canvas rendering at 2x scale mapping the absolute layout bounding box with zero empty margins
State Engine: 50 levels of Undo/Redo stack, copy/paste/duplicate clipboards, and local storage auto-save thread

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
StickDiagram is an interactive web EDA editor that enables engineers to sketch, inspect, and share VLSI stick layouts.

The app runs entirely client-side using React 19 and a raw HTML5 Canvas context 2D. Wires, contacts, and labels snap to pitch layouts, and horizontal/vertical metal intersections automatically create bridge loops unless manually joined.

sections.title: VLSI Design & Auto-Bridging
sections.content:
Traditional VLSI diagrams represent different layers (diffusion, polysilicon, metal1, metal2). Wires on different layers cross without connecting. Wires on the same layer, however, would short-circuit unless an insulation bridge is explicitly drawn.

StickDiagram automates this rule: when orthogonal lines sharing the same layer intersect, the vertical path remains straight while the horizontal path segments around it with an arc bridge. Right-clicking the bridge registers the intersection as an override, turning it into a solid junction.

sections.title: Custom Double-Pass LaTeX Renderer
sections.content: To render node labels like VDD and GND with proper mathematical subscripts (e.g. V_{DD}, V_SS), the canvas context uses a regex parser. It splits label strings into base and subtext, drawing the base in an italic serif font, calculating its text metrics, and offset-drawing the subtext in a smaller size just to its right.

sections.title: System Architecture
sections.caption: StickDiagram Model-View-Controller Front-End Flow.
sections.content: The application is built on a clean front-end MVC model. React handles the document model state (elements, layer stack, undo/redo, selected IDs), the CanvasArea receives user inputs and coordinates them through viewport transforms, and raw HTML5 canvas rendering functions paint the world coordinates onto screen pixels at up to 60fps.

sections.title: Scope & Commands
items: HTML5 Canvas Context 2D for pixel-perfect VLSI layout features
Infinite 2D Workspace scaling (0.1x to 5.0x zoom) and coordinate translation
Photoshop-like layer management: support visibility, lock toggles, and customized opacity sliders
File system operations: Save Project (.stk) and Open Project via serialized JSON files
Hotkeys support: V (Select), W (Wire), P (Via), L (Label), B (Brush), E (Eraser), Ctrl+Z (Undo), Ctrl+Y (Redo)

sections.title: My Role
sections.content: Solo Creator & Lead Developer — designed the canvas coordinate transformations, developed the auto-bridging intersection solver, built the custom LaTeX-style subscript renderer, and styled the dark/light mode responsive layout.

title: Plant.io — 8-Bit IoT Nursery Dashboard
description: Cross-platform Flutter dashboard for ESP32-based plant nurseries: real-time sensor telemetry, actuator control, plant-mood scoring, and an 8-bit retro UI.
fullDescription:
Plant.io (a.k.a. the Onion Nursery Monitoring App) is a Flutter-based IoT monitoring dashboard built for nurseries, greenhouses, and hobby garden beds. It pairs with ESP32 devices to deliver real-time telemetry — soil moisture, temperature, humidity, EC, and pH — alongside remote actuator control (pump, fans, fertilizer dispensing), a historical log timeline, weather integration, and a plant-health scoring system rendered as an animated "onion" avatar.

The app is structured in layers (presentation → state → business logic → data) using Provider for reactive state and Firebase Realtime Database + Cloud Firestore for live sensor data and historical alerts. The UI commits fully to an 8-bit aesthetic: Press Start 2P typography, CRT scanline effects, pixel-art borders, and retro sound design for UI feedback.

This is a freelance engagement. Per contract, screenshots, client branding, and certain implementation details are withheld from the public case study.
highlights: Real-time multi-device monitoring: soil moisture, temperature, humidity, EC, pH
Remote actuator control: pump, fans, and fertilizer dispensing per bed
Plant-mood scoring renders live health via an animated onion avatar
Historical analytics with fl_chart line/pie/bar visualizations
Weather integration (Open-Meteo) + location-aware recommendations
Offline caching, connectivity monitoring, and local push alerts (thirsty/hot/critical)
Cross-platform: iOS, Android, Web, macOS, Linux, Windows

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Plant.io is a production-style IoT dashboard for ESP32 garden devices. The system continuously streams sensor telemetry into Firebase, computes a plant-health "mood", and lets the user actuate pumps, fans, and a fertilizer dispenser remotely.

The visual language is deliberate: an 8-bit dark-mode UI with Press Start 2P typography, CRT scanlines, pixelated components, and a custom animated plant avatar that reflects the computed mood in real time.

sections.title: Architecture
paragraphs: The app follows a layered architecture with Provider-based state management. The presentation layer (screens + widgets) is driven by ChangeNotifier providers that sit over a business logic layer (services, analytics, calculations), which in turn sits over a data layer (models, Firebase, SharedPreferences).

Core design patterns in use: Singleton (FirebaseService, NotificationService, SoundService, ConnectivityService), Provider/Observer (reactive UI updates), Factory (fromMap/fromJson on all models), Strategy (normal, randomAuto, and custom data modes), and Builder (Scaffold/RichText composition).

State is split across two primary providers — NurseryProvider for device and sensor state, and WeatherProvider for location and weather context — while services mediate all external I/O: Firebase, notifications, audio feedback, and connectivity.

sections.title: Key Features
items: Multiple garden beds / ESP32 devices monitored simultaneously
Real-time telemetry: soil moisture, temperature, humidity, EC, pH
Remote actuation: pump, fans, fertilizer dispensing with per-bed commands
Historical logging and trend analysis with fl_chart
Plant mood indicator with animated onion avatar (thirsty / hot / critical / thriving)
Weather integration via Open-Meteo for environmental context
Offline support with SharedPreferences caching and connectivity awareness
Local push notifications for critical plant conditions (no internet required)
Retro 8-bit UI: Press Start 2P fonts, CRT scanline effect, pixel borders, and retro SFX

sections.title: Hardware Integration
sections.content: The app pairs with ESP32 firmware over Firebase Realtime Database: devices push sensor readings to per-device streams and subscribe to a command channel for actuator control. The repository ships an ESP32 simulator (simulate_esp32.js) so the dashboard can be developed and demoed without physical hardware on bench.

sections.title: Design & UX
paragraphs: The 8-bit retro direction is implemented consistently across the UI: Press Start 2P for typography, a CRT scanline overlay widget, pixelated borders, and a palette optimized for a dark gardening terminal feel. Audio feedback (click, alert, success) is pre-generated and played through audioplayers to reinforce the retro tone.
The centerpiece is the animated onion avatar — a custom-painted plant character that visualizes the computed plant mood in real time, backed by a pixel-art garden scene.

sections.title: Confidentiality
sections.content: Plant.io is a freelance engagement. Under the client contract, screenshots, full UI walkthroughs, and some implementation details are withheld. The description above is limited to non-sensitive architectural and feature-level information.

title: The Safehouse — Community Showcase & Pitch Site
description: Narrative showcase and pitch website for The Safehouse community, built around an interactive 3D scroll journey — campfire, forest, tent, and signposts — powered by React Three Fiber, GSAP, and Lenis smooth scroll.
fullDescription:
The Safehouse website is a showcase and pitch site for the community built around the idea "A Home Built on People." The website itself is not a product, app, or onboarding platform — it is a public-facing marketing/brand page used to introduce visitors, partners, and prospective members to what the community is about. The goal is storytelling and positioning, not transactions.

Instead of a conventional static landing page, the site is delivered as a guided 3D scroll journey (campfire gathering, forest path, signposts, and a gate). Visitors "walk" through the brand narrative as they scroll, with each section tied to a waypoint in the scene.

Under the hood, the site is a React 19 single-page app built with Vite and Tailwind CSS 4. A React Three Fiber scene drives the journey world, GSAP + Lenis handle scroll-driven choreography, and Rive + Framer Motion add vector and component-level motion. The narrative is sliced into ~13 scroll sections (Hero, Mission, StandFor, TrackRecord, WhyWorks, Offer, WhyPartner, Opportunities, Beyond, Finale, AboutUs, Team, Socials, Contacts) each tied to scroll stages and waypoint reveals.

Accessibility is a first-class concern: a MotionModeContext respects prefers-reduced-motion and provides fallback animations, and the heavy JourneyCanvas is lazy-loaded to keep initial load light.

gallery.caption: Hero section — full-width landing and brand frame

highlights: Interactive 3D journey scene (forest → signposts → gate → campfire gathering) in React Three Fiber
GSAP timelines + ScrollTrigger wired to Lenis smooth scroll via a shared Tempus clock
13 scroll-driven narrative sections, each tied to waypoints and scroll stages
Rive vector animations + SplitHeading / Reveal / Parallax primitives for component-level motion
Procedural terrain (CurvedGround), ember particles, and path-curve math for the journey world
Accessibility-first: MotionModeContext respects prefers-reduced-motion, JourneyCanvas lazy-loaded

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
This project is the showcase and pitch website for The Safehouse community. It is purely a marketing/brand site — not a product, app, or onboarding platform. Visitors arrive at a Hero with hanging elements, follow a scroll-driven path through mission, values, offerings, opportunities, and team sections, and exit through a Finale/Contacts hand-off.

The canonical metaphor — a campfire gathering in a forest clearing, approached by following signposts through a curved valley — is rendered in real-time WebGL and drives the pacing of the text sections around it.

sections.title: The 3D Journey
paragraphs: The hero and mission stages are anchored by a CampfireCanvas — a React Three Fiber scene with a campfire, embers (custom particle system), tent, and camera rig that subtly drifts around the gathering. Post-processing effects (@react-three/postprocessing) add bloom around the fire to sell the warmth.

As the user scrolls, a second, larger JourneyCanvas takes over: a procedurally generated curved ground (CurvedGround + groundMath + pathCurve), a grass field, a tree forest, birds, and themed signposts (Sign + signTexture + JourneySigns) along a path strip that ends at a gate. A JourneyCamera rig follows the path, driven by scroll progress rather than time.
Both canvases are gated behind a JourneyScrollBridge and a MotionModeContext check so users who prefer reduced motion see static fallbacks instead of real-time 3D.

sections.title: Narrative Sections
sections.content: The experience is split into purpose-built section components under src/sections/, each self-contained and tied to a scroll stage:
items: Hero / HangingHero — opening frame with hanging elements and split-text animation
Mission — what The Safehouse is and why it exists
WhyWorks — explanation of the onboarding model
Beyond / Finale — closing narrative and hand-off
AboutUs, Team, Socials, Contacts — organizational footer surfaces

sections.title: Component Architecture
paragraphs: Page sections (src/sections/) are top-level and scroll-anchored. Reusable UI primitives (src/components/) include Nav, Parallax, Reveal, RoughCard, ScrollStages, SplitHeading, Waypoint, and JourneyScrollBridge — small pieces that handle one concern each and compose cleanly inside section components.

The 3D layer (src/three/ and src/three/journey/) is split into canvases (CampfireCanvas, JourneyCanvas), scenes (CampfireScene, JourneyWorld), entities (Tent, Tree, Birds, Gate, Embers), camera rigs (CameraRig, JourneyCamera), and pure math/RNG modules (groundMath, pathCurve, fireConstants, rng, signTexture) so the scenes stay deterministic and tree-shakeable.

sections.title: Performance & Accessibility
sections.content: JourneyCanvas is loaded via React.lazy to keep initial bundle size manageable despite the 3D payload. GSAP animations stick to GPU-accelerated transform + opacity. Lenis drives smooth scroll without replacing native scroll semantics. MotionModeContext surfaces user motion preferences to every scene and animation primitive.

sections.title: Key Features
items: Silk-smooth scrolling via Lenis, synchronized with GSAP timelines through Tempus
Scroll-triggered reveals, parallax, staggered entrances, and split-text headings
Interactive 3D journey visualization with campfire, forest, path, gate, birds, and embers
Procedural terrain generation and deterministic RNG for repeatable scenes
Rive vector animation integration for interactive accent elements
Motion preferences respected throughout via MotionModeContext
Multi-page routing with scroll-to-top on navigation and deep-linking support

sections.title: Status
sections.content: This is a showcase website, not a product. It is actively in development (April 2026): the 3D journey, scroll choreography, and narrative sections are in place; polish, final copy, and production deployment are still ongoing.

title: Student Consultation System
description: A modern, full-stack web application for managing student-professor consultations with real-time messaging, scheduling, and comprehensive user management.
fullDescription:
The Student Consultation System is a full-stack web platform that turns ad-hoc student–professor consultations into a structured, trackable workflow.

It features role-based interfaces with real-time messaging, an interactive scheduling calendar, consultation request management, and a notification system. A three-tier architecture separates the React frontend, an Express API, and a PostgreSQL database. Features include professor discovery, consultation booking with a clear status lifecycle (pending → approved → ongoing → completed), file attachments, profile management with photo cropping, dashboard analytics, and announcement posting.

Security and performance: JWT authentication, bcrypt password hashing, parameterized PostgreSQL queries, and a Sharp-based image pipeline for profile photos.

Note: client project — screenshots are not available per a confidentiality agreement.
highlights: Role-based interfaces for students, professors, and admins
Real-time messaging and interactive scheduling calendar
JWT auth, bcrypt hashing, and Sharp-based image processing
Dashboard analytics and announcement posting
Client project — screenshots not available per NDA

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Students discover professors, book consultations with a clear status lifecycle, and communicate through real-time messaging with attachments. Professors manage availability and requests; admins oversee users and announcements.

sections.title: Security & Performance
items: Sharp-based image pipeline for profile photos
Response-level caching on the hottest read endpoints

sections.title: Confidentiality
sections.content: This is a freelance engagement. Per contract, screenshots, client-facing copy, final assets, and some implementation details are withheld from the public case study. The case study above is limited to non-sensitive architectural and feature-level information.

title: Walang Basagan ng Thrift
description: Y2K-themed thrift e-commerce platform with a React + TypeScript storefront, Express backend, role-based auth, admin dashboard, and a built-in customer support inbox.
fullDescription:
Walang Basagan ng Thrift is a Y2K-inspired thrift store web application built with React 19, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and an Express backend. It combines an online storefront, role-based authentication, a shopping flow, customer support threads, and an admin dashboard for managing users, products, orders, homepage content, and support tickets.

The storefront emphasizes branded visual storytelling — a Y2K aesthetic with pink-and-white palette, rounded UI, background music, and section-based homepage composition. The backend supports both SQLite (default) and a JSON fallback mode so the project runs cleanly on Windows developer machines even without a native SQLite toolchain.

This is a freelance engagement. Per contract, screenshots and certain implementation details are withheld from the public case study; the description here covers only non-sensitive architecture and feature-level information.
highlights: Full storefront: home, product catalog, detail pages, cart, checkout, and order history
Role-based auth (admin / moderator / buyer) with protected routes and session redirects
Admin dashboard: users, products, orders, support tickets, homepage content, reviews
In-app support inbox: threaded messages with moderator/admin assignment
SQLite-first storage with JSON fallback mode for Windows without native build tools
Image uploads via multer, served from /uploads with homepage-driven content model

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Walang Basagan ng Thrift is a full-stack thrift e-commerce platform designed around a Y2K visual identity. Customers can browse curated products, add to cart, check out, and track orders; admins and moderators run the store through a dedicated back-office dashboard with product, order, user, support, and homepage management.

The architecture is deliberately portable: the frontend is a Vite + React + TypeScript SPA, and the backend is a single Express process that can run either against better-sqlite3 or a JSON file store, chosen via an environment flag.

sections.title: Key Features
items: Branded Y2K home page with hero, promos, product showcase, brand/trust sections, reviews, newsletter, footer, background music, and a support widget
Authentication with role-aware session handling, password visibility toggle, and role-based post-login redirects
Shopping flow: product listing, product detail, add-to-cart, buy now, cart management, checkout, order history
Customer support: buyer-facing support widget, threaded messaging, moderator/admin reply tools, and thread assignment
Admin dashboard: user management, product CRUD, order management, homepage content editing, support ticket handling, review monitoring, role/status controls
Content safety: the frontend merges backend homepage content with defaults so the site still renders if fields are missing

sections.title: User Roles
sections.content: Permissions follow a strict three-tier model. The first admin account is specifically protected from demotion or status change to guarantee a working back door for recovery.

groups.label: Admin
items: First admin is protected from role / status changes

sections.title: Routing & API
items: Buyer: POST /api/orders, GET /api/orders, PATCH /api/orders/:orderId/cancel, support threads under /api/support
Admin: full CRUD on users, products, orders, homepage, and support, plus homepage uploads

sections.title: Security & Access Control
items: Passwords hashed with bcryptjs before storage
Admin routes require an X-User-Id header
Role checks block unauthorized access to privileged routes
Suspended and banned users are rejected at login and on protected actions
First admin account is locked from demotion or status changes

sections.title: Confidentiality
sections.content: This is a freelance engagement. Per contract, screenshots, client-facing copy, final assets, and some implementation details are withheld from the public case study. The case study above is limited to non-sensitive architectural and feature-level information.

title: EMG Interface Controller
description: Real-time electromyography (EMG) signal processing and visualization system for muscle activity monitoring and human-computer interaction.
fullDescription:
The EMG Controller is a real-time electromyography signal processing and visualization system built on the ESP32-S3. It captures, processes, and visualizes EMG signals with professional-grade accuracy and provides multiple interaction modes, including game control and data analysis.

The firmware samples at 500 Hz, runs a multi-stage DSP pipeline, and streams the processed signal over UART. On the PC side, a Python GUI visualizes the signal in real time, the Arduino Serial Plotter is supported for quick debugging, and a pynput-based bridge can drive a Roblox game controller.

Custom hardware includes a KiCad-designed PCB and a Fusion 360 3D-printed enclosure for professional integration.
highlights: 500 Hz EMG sampling on ESP32-S3
Adaptive threshold calibration per user and muscle group
Real-time Python GUI + Arduino serial plotter visualization
Game controller bridge with Roblox integration (pynput)
Custom KiCad PCB + Fusion 360 3D-printed enclosure

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: A real-time EMG acquisition and visualization platform on the ESP32-S3. The system captures muscle activity, applies a multi-stage DSP pipeline, and drives two downstream use cases: a scientific visualization GUI and a game controller bridge.

sections.title: Signal Processing Pipeline
sections.caption: End-to-end flow: electrodes → EMG Candy front-end → ESP32-S3 ADC → DSP stages → UART → PC outputs.
sections.content: The pipeline runs entirely on the ESP32-S3 and emits a clean, thresholded envelope over UART at 115200 baud. Adaptive threshold calibration adjusts per session so the same firmware behaves correctly across users and muscle groups.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.content: Custom KiCad-designed PCB integrating the Upside Down Labs EMG Candy front-end with an ESP32-S3 core. A Fusion 360 3D-printed enclosure houses the PCB and provides strain relief for the EMG leads.

sections.title: Applications
items: Real-time Python GUI for research-grade visualization
Arduino Serial Plotter for quick debugging
Game controller bridge (Roblox integration via pynput)

title: Manhwa Reader
description: React + TypeScript SPA to browse and read manhwa via the MangaDex API—discovery, detail pages, and a full chapter reader with dark/light themes.
fullDescription:
Manhwa Reader is a single-page web app for discovering titles and reading chapters end-to-end: home with search, popular and recently updated feeds, manhwa detail (cover, synopsis, English chapter list), and a dedicated reader with page images, chapter selector, and previous/next navigation.

The stack is React 19, TypeScript, Vite 7, React Router DOM 7, and Tailwind CSS 3, with all manga data coming from the MangaDex REST API. Theme preference persists via localStorage; the UI includes loading, empty, and error states so inconsistent API payloads don’t break the flow.

Chapter lists merge paginated results, filter duplicate chapter numbers, and prefer entries with better page coverage. Page URLs are resolved from MangaDex base URL, chapter hash, and filenames for reliable image loading.
highlights: Home: hero search, popular & recently updated grids, category chips
Title search against MangaDex with result grid → detail → reader flow
Detail: cover, synopsis, English chapters, start reading
Reader: full chapter images, chapter dropdown, prev/next, optional control panel toggle
Settings: light/dark theme with persistence
Robust handling of missing fields, duplicate chapters, and empty/error states

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Core flow: Home → Manhwa detail → Chapter reader. The app emphasizes a calm reading layout, responsive grids for discovery, and sticky reader chrome so navigation stays reachable during long sessions.

sections.title: Architecture
paragraphs: Components under src/components/ (e.g. Navbar, ManhwaCard), route-level pages under src/pages/ (Home, ManhwaDetail, Reader, Settings).
src/services/mangaService.ts centralizes API calls and data shaping; src/config/api.ts holds endpoints and URL helpers.
ThemeContext + useTheme wire global light/dark mode; src/types/ carries typed models for manga, chapters, and card props; src/utils/ handles helpers such as date formatting.

sections.title: MangaDex integration
items: Popular and recently updated feeds; search by title
Manga metadata by ID; English chapter feed (paginated, merged)
Chapter pages from baseUrl + hash + filename
Duplicate chapter numbers filtered; richer page-count entries preferred

sections.title: Key features
items: Discovery grids, search, and navigation into detail and reader
Full chapter image loading with chapter selector and prev/next
Loading spinners and not-found / no-pages fallbacks

sections.title: Challenges & approach
sections.content: MangaDex payloads can be incomplete or inconsistent across uploads. The client uses defensive defaults for titles and descriptions, deduplicates chapter rows, and keeps the reader usable with explicit empty and error UI. For long reading sessions, controls can be toggled for a cleaner viewport.

title: Aira.sys Personal Portfolio
description: This portfolio site — a dashboard-style resume built with React, TypeScript, and Tailwind, themed as a terminal OS.
fullDescription:
A personal portfolio and resume site built as a terminal-inspired dashboard.

Features include a fixed sidebar navigation, scroll-spy section highlighting, a 3D project carousel, a standalone portfolio grid page, and per-project detail pages with inline architecture diagrams. Entirely static, deployed to GitHub Pages via the gh-pages workflow.
highlights: Terminal-OS visual language with sidebar + system header
3D project carousel with perspective transforms
Standalone portfolio grid and per-project detail pages
Inline SVG architecture diagrams on case studies
Static build deployed to GitHub Pages

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: This site is both the resume you are reading and a design exercise — a lightweight dashboard UI themed as a terminal OS, built as a single-page React app with hash-based routing for the portfolio subsection.

sections.title: Key Features
items: Standalone portfolio grid with per-project detail pages
Inline SVG architecture and pipeline diagrams
Resume PDF download with floating CTA

title: Fehuvia — Open Finance Web3 B2B SME Treasury Workstation
description: High-fidelity Web3 B2B treasury workstation on Morph Layer 2 featuring an AI OCR automated invoice digitization scanner, an AI co-pilot engine (GPT-4o via context-aware RAG), and an autonomous gas faucet.
fullDescription:
Fehuvia is a high-fidelity Web3 B2B treasury workstation engineered to prevent liquidity dryouts and multi-day corporate settlement delays for Southeast Asian SMEs. By marrying relational database state tracking with next-generation smart contract execution rails on Morph Layer 2, Fehuvia slashes settlement times from a 3-day banking standard to under 2 seconds (T+0) while optimizing transaction costs to a fraction of a cent.

Equipped with an AI Financial Co-Pilot (GPT-4o via context-aware RAG) and Automated AI OCR Invoice Digitization, the platform parses incoming PDF/image invoices, automatically populates billing drawers, maps suppliers, and classifies obligations into actionable runway statuses (Safe to Pay, Delay, or Review) based on projected cashflow reserves.

To resolve the core Web3 onboarding obstacle—transaction gas acquisition—Fehuvia runs an automated background faucet (/api/faucet/drip) that monitors operator MetaMask accounts and dispenses L2 ETH asynchronously as needed. A reliable off-chain listener daemon tracks EVM log event streams to ensure a highly audited, bulletproof dual-state relational database ledger in PostgreSQL.
highlights: Web3 Settlements: Atomic sub-2 second payment clearance on Morph Layer 2 via custom Solidity contracts
EVM Listener Daemon: Real-time logs syncing to PostgreSQL for robust dual-state financial auditing
AI Financial Co-Pilot: GPT-4o RAG pipeline for structured cashflow analytics and runway forecasting
AI OCR Scan: Auto-character grid parsing for uploaded invoice sheets, auto-filling workstation drawers
Frictionless Faucet: Self-healing background gas faucet dispensing L2 gas asynchronously to MetaMask accounts
Zero-Custody: Client-side signing via MetaMask (EIP-1193), keeping private keys completely out of servers

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Fehuvia bridges B2B blockchain transparency and sub-second payment clearance with traditional relational database speed and analytics.

The system is built with React 19 and Vite for a custom glassmorphic workstation UI, an Express.js backend for AI orchestration and database sync, PostgreSQL for transactional persistence, and Solidity smart contracts compiled with Hardhat and deployed to Morph L2 Testnet.

sections.title: Feature Highlights & Video Showcase
sections.caption: Video highlighting Fehuvia's core workstation features and design.
sections.content: This video highlights the core features of the Fehuvia SME workstation, showing the user interface, design, and key functionalities.

sections.title: System Architecture
sections.caption: Layered flow: OCR scan -> PostgreSQL draft -> MetaMask L2 signature -> EVM execution -> Listener daemon dual-state reconciliation.
sections.content: Fehuvia's architecture operates in five modular phases: first, AI OCR processes invoice uploads; second, Express stores invoice drafts in PostgreSQL; third, the CFO signs transactions client-side using MetaMask; fourth, Morph L2 settles payments atomically; and fifth, a background node Listener Daemon captures smart contract event emissions and synchronizes the Postgres database state.

sections.title: Technology Stack
sections.content: A high-performance stack separating client-side zero-custody signature from heavy analytics and immutable Web3 settlements.

sections.title: Smart Contract: B2BSettlement.sol
sections.content: Our central decentralized billing clearance protocol enforces atomic, non-custodial transactions directly on Morph L2:
items: Implements stablecoin (mUSDC ERC-20) transfers mapping atomic clearance status to invoice UUIDs
Employs the Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern strictly to block double-spend and reentrancy exploits
Emits PaymentSettled logs carrying invoice, buyer, supplier, amount, and block timestamp telemetry

sections.title: Background Gas Faucet Parameters
sections.content: Configuration variables utilized by the self-healing faucet node to eliminate onboarding barriers for non-crypto operators.

sections.title: Zero-Custody Principles
sections.content: Security is paramount: the Fehuvia API server has zero access to user seed phrases or private keys. The backend only manages a gas dispenser vault key; actual supplier-bound settlements are signed exclusively in the browser using the EIP-1193 provider injection standard.
